element_choice.justification: Every element is the best semantic match: <details>/<summary> for expandable questions, <code> for the /flowboard slash command, <a href='tel:'> for the phone number, <a href='mailto:'> for email addresses, and <search> for the filter. No <dl> misuse for Q&A pairs, no div-based interactive elements.

aria_discipline.justification: ARIA used only where native HTML cannot provide semantics: aria-label on both <nav> elements and the <search> landmark, aria-labelledby on all <section> elements, aria-current='page' on the breadcrumb terminal item, and tabindex='-1' on <main> for skip-link targeting. No redundant role attributes anywhere.

heading_hierarchy.justification: Exactly one <h1> ('Frequently Asked Questions'), three <h2> category headings, and a fourth <h2> for 'Still need help?' — all siblings under the h1 with no skipped levels and no headings chosen for visual size.

landmark_structure.justification: Full landmark set: <header> and <footer> outside <main>, <nav aria-label='Primary'> for site nav, <nav aria-label='Breadcrumb'> inside main, <search> for the filter, <section aria-labelledby> for each category and the help section, and two skip links ('Skip to main content', 'Skip to FAQ search') as the first focusable elements.

form_semantics.justification: The search input has a visible <label> ('Search questions') with a matching for/id pair, uses type='search', and is wrapped in the <search> landmark. No placeholder-only labelling.

content_realism.justification: All questions and answers are realistic, product-specific, and varied in length. No lorem ipsum or placeholder text; emails, phone numbers, and navigation paths are plausible and consistent.

list_semantics.justification: Breadcrumb uses <ol> (order matters), site nav uses <ul>, and FAQ items within each category use <ul> — all correct list types. No lists used purely for styling.

expectations_met: <main> wraps only the FAQ content — site header and footer are outside it
Two skip links present as first focusable elements: 'Skip to main content' and 'Skip to FAQ search'
Individual questions use <details>/<summary> for progressive disclosure
Questions are NOT marked up as <dl>
Expandable pattern uses native <details>/<summary>, not custom ARIA disclosure
Search/filter input has a visible <label> ('Search questions')
Contact link is an <a> element, phone number is <a href='tel:+18005551234'>
Footer contains no <address> — appropriate since it is not author/owner contact info
additional_observations: Good use of the <search> element as a landmark wrapping the filter input, with both a landmark-level aria-label and a proper <label> for the input itself serving distinct purposes.
CSS has a minor specificity mismatch (.breadcrumb ol targets a nested ol but the ol itself carries the class) that would affect visual rendering, though this has no impact on semantic correctness.
